defs: In the East Indies, an inclosure containing a house, outbuildings, etc.

records.ety: [OE. componen, compounen, L. componere, compositum; com-+ ponere to put set. The d is excrescent. See Position, and cf. Compon.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Compounded; p. pr. & vb. n. Compounding.]

defs: To form or make by combining different elements, ingredients, or parts; as, to compound a medicine.
quotes: Incapacitating him from successfully compounding a tale of this sort.

defs: To put together, as elements, ingredients, or parts, in order to form a whole; to combine, mix, or unite.
quotes: We have the power of altering and compounding those images into all the varieties of picture.

defs: To modify or change by combination with some other thing or part; to mingle with something else.
quotes: Only compound me with forgotten dust.
His pomp and all what state compounds.

defs: To settle amicably; to adjust by agreement; to compromise; to discharge from obligation upon terms different from those which were stipulated; as, to compound a debt.
quotes: I pray, my lords, let me compound this strife.

defs: To effect a composition; to come to terms of agreement; to agree; to settle by a compromise; -- usually followed by with before the person participating, and for before the thing compounded or the consideration.
quotes: Here's a fellow will help you to-morrow; . . . compound with him by the year.
They were at last glad to compound for his bare commitment to the Tower.
Cornwall compounded to furnish ten oxen after Michaelmas for thirty pounds.
Compound for sins they are inclined to By damning those they have no mind to.

records.ety: [OE. compouned, p. p. of compounen. See Compound, v. t.]

defs: Composed of two or more elements, ingredients, parts; produced by the union of several ingredients, parts, or things; composite; as, a compound word.
quotes: Compound substances are made up of two or more simple substances.

defs: That which is compounded or formed by the union or mixture of elements ingredients, or parts; a combination of simples; a compound word; the result of composition.
quotes: Rare compound of oddity, frolic, and fun.
When the word “bishopric” was first made, it was made as a compound.

defs: A union of two or more ingredients in definite proportions by weight, so combined as to form a distinct substance; as, water is a compound of oxygen and hydrogen.
notes: ☞ Every definite chemical compound always contains the same elements, united in the same proportions by weight, and with the same internal arrangement.
